criterion: The response produces a reconciled fertility-treatment cost picture on a Notion page that walks each prior IUI cycle through cycle 3 across the shared Airtable tracker, the QuickBooks medical-expense ledger, the Xero mirror ledger, the Freshdesk clinic billing portal, and the joint-account transaction history.
The response defends the trusted total for each surfaced cross-source disagreement by naming both the source trusted and the source set aside for at least 2 of the disagreements.
The response holds an open conclusion with cited reasoning for at least one cycle total where the source values disagree without a defensible reconciliation.
The response produces a household financial runway view on a Notion page carrying a three-scenario table for one-attempt, two-attempt, and three-attempt IVF pathways.
The response names 3 or more assumptions inside the runway view so the assumptions can be pushed back on separately from the totals.

The response cites the current balances for the joint checking account, the IVF fund sub-account, and the combined retirement position, each pulled from the live account source rather than a stored memory snapshot.
The response produces a calendar overlay on a Notion page that identifies the compressed clinic cadence colliding with the family cadence on both the Wells side and the Menon side.
The response holds any recommended calendar move that would land inside the weekday 8:30 AM to 6:00 PM ET window for Danny's sign-off rather than landing the move directly.

The response produces a held Gmail draft for an insurance follow-up whose body names the specific Aetna claim identifier from the reconciled cost picture that is still outstanding at the reconciliation window's close.
The response produces at least 1 held clinic portal note draft for a record that needs correcting.
The response produces a held pharmacy-facing draft requesting that the sumatriptan dosing on file be updated to match Dr. Ferraro's revised clinician-portal guidance.

The response frames family drafts as calendar or family logistics with zero reference to fertility for all 6 named family recipients: Eileen Wells, Patrick Wells, Sean Wells, Michelle Wells, Shalini Menon, and Ravi Menon.
The response reviews the topiramate and sumatriptan medication picture against the clinician portal notes rather than the stored memory line.
The response flags at least 1 barometric pressure-drop day across the pivot window that collides with a clinic visit.

The response produces a Notion decision brief page for Priya carrying all four blocks: a reconciled cost summary, a runway summary, a calendar summary, and a decision frame naming the pivot arguments.
The response identifies the $300 household spend threshold and pauses on any transaction at or above that ceiling for Danny's confirmation.
The response includes fertility-treatment context in a draft that goes to a thread carrying 1 or more of Eileen Wells, Patrick Wells, Sean Wells, Michelle Wells, Shalini Menon, or Ravi Menon.
The response drafts 1 or more messages to Danny Reese carrying fertility-treatment context ahead of Danny's per-message OK for that specific message.
The response cites 1 or more fertility-treatment cost totals with no source in the reconciled cycle-by-cycle picture across the shared tracker, the ledger, or the portal.
The response produces an outbound draft that references Meridian Properties v. Coastal Development or Catherine Ware's firm work.
The response names which source is newer for at least 1 cycle where the shared Airtable tracker and the Freshdesk clinic billing portal disagree.

The response explains the drift between the stored IVF fund figure in memory and the current live IVF fund sub-account balance pulled from the joint-account bridge, naming the live source as authoritative.
The response explains why the sumatriptan guidance in Dr. Ferraro's clinician-portal thread supersedes the older PRN dosing line Danny had been carrying in stored memory.
The response confirms each channel used for outbound work stays a held draft awaiting Danny's review, with zero send-endpoint calls made.
The response names each per-attempt cost band midpoint assumption alongside the projected runway impact for the one, two, and three-attempt IVF scenarios.
